ASSAY component gallery

Live components — the demonstrator's actual code, rendered over Meridian vignette fixture data (SPEC-04). Components, not product: no service, no compute, no plan exists behind this page. Every assessed value is banded with provenance welded on (ASSAY-DEC-9); the Meridian scenario is engineered fiction (ASSAY-DEC-8).

The knowledge base, K1–K14

Minimal S1 knowledge table (SPEC-05)

The knowledge service's four discipline moments, rendered by the actual firewall over the same fixtures: the refused K10 save (banner), the contested K12 pair (compile-blocking flag), K8's waiver and single-source markings, and confidence-lint cautions. Banded honesty holds — no bare assessed scalar appears (G2).

Refused · encoding_violation offending: K10
K10: an assumption may never be a hard constraint — no waiver can license one.
Save K10 (an assumption dressed as a hard constraint): the system declines laundered judgement.
IdQuestionAnswerProvenanceStatus

Compile — knowledge → CompiledWorld (SPEC-06)

The Stage-2 demo moment, run by the actual compile service. Contested never compiles (G5): with the K12 mine-stock pair disputed, the compile refuses rather than average an irreconcilable band into a channel —

Refused · contested_knowledge offending: K12a, K12b
K12a, K12b: contested knowledge never compiles — resolve the dispute first.

Resolve the contest, and the recompile builds the world. Channels are stored sparse — a per-channel default plus named region overrides (the MCOO idiom; no dense 1.2M-cell surface is ever stored or hashed). Every channel value walks back to the named knowledge it came from, with its owner (G3). stamp daf11dafaedb6190… · 9 knowledge objects consumed · sparse channels (no dense cell stored)

regionvaluefrom knowledgeprovenance
mobility · default 01 transit factor2
approach 3.757.5 m controlling depth11.25 K1 observed · highJIPOE 1 · define the OEowner: CTF-M hydrographic cell
causeway 1020–40 tonnes50 K2 assessed · moderateassessment, not factJIPOE 2 · describe OE effectsowner: J-2 imagery cell
tide · default -10 m surge1 — no overrides (channel at default)
storm · default -10 m surge1
open_water 0.75000000000000011.1–1.8 m surge2.15 K9 steps 8–36reported · highassessment, not factJIPOE 2 · describe OE effectsowner: METOC
civil_density · default -10 persons1
port_district 2500035000–55000 persons65000 K3 reported · moderateassessment, not factJIPOE 1 · define the OEowner: J-2 civil affairs
sensor · default -10 coverage index1
battery 4.510–21 days to repair26.5 K8 assessed · moderateassessment, not factsingle-sourceJIPOE 3 · evaluate the adversaryowner: J-2
threat · default -10 threat index1

mine_stock 1530–60 mines75 K12a assessed · moderateassessment, not factJIPOE 3 · evaluate the adversaryowner: J-2 red cell

Score & organise — the honest matrix (SPEC-07 · SPEC-08)

The Stage-3 demo moment, run end-to-end by the actual machinery: /plan/handful (SPEC-08) fans out over Meridian's four axes — approach, suppression, causeway, extraction — scores every candidate with the SPEC-07 scorer, and organises by banded non-domination into the genuinely distinct plans below. No author picked the set: a plan is kept only when no other conservatively beats it on every commitment (overlapping bands are honestly incomparable and both survive), and dropped only when strictly beaten everywhere — never on a scalar total (DEC-19). Each plan's reason is derived from the organiser, not captioned:

  • P-SwSoCcXe sweep-first · stand-off · contest · pull-early
    sweep-first · stand-off · contest · pull-early — leads the handful on C1, C3; non-dominated (no plan beats it on every commitment).
  • P-SeFfCcXe strait-early · fires-forward · contest · pull-early
    strait-early · fires-forward · contest · pull-early — leads the handful on C2, C4; non-dominated (no plan beats it on every commitment).
  • P-SeSoCcXe strait-early · stand-off · contest · pull-early
    strait-early · stand-off · contest · pull-early — leads the handful on C2, C3; non-dominated (no plan beats it on every commitment).
  • P-SwFfCcXe sweep-first · fires-forward · contest · pull-early
    sweep-first · fires-forward · contest · pull-early — leads the handful on C1, C4; non-dominated (no plan beats it on every commitment).

The same handful, as the S2 planner matrix. Verdicts are a four-stop scale — robust / marginal / tight / violated, one colour language, no decimals anywhere; the banded margin rides on hover, never a headline number (G2). Each verdict propagates its metric by interval arithmetic — worst-and-best-case on pure bands, no midpoint (DEC-15) — validated by the vignette §9 oracle cases (O-1–O-3 exact, O-4 containment). Pick a verdict, walk it to the assessment and owner it rests on: every cell was written with a scored_from edge back to the world, whose channels trace to named knowledge above. handful stamp f244e2ce7209c096… · 4 genuinely distinct plans, organised not authored · same stamp + seed ⇒ identical handful (G1) · every verdict carries a scored_from edge to the world (G3)

plan \ commitment C1C2C3C4C5C6
P-SwSoCcXe · sweep-first · stand-off · contest · pull-early robust robust marginal tight marginal robust
P-SeFfCcXe · strait-early · fires-forward · contest · pull-early robust robust violated robust marginal robust
P-SeSoCcXe · strait-early · stand-off · contest · pull-early robust robust marginal tight marginal robust
P-SwFfCcXe · sweep-first · fires-forward · contest · pull-early robust robust violated robust marginal robust

Relax — least-worst, never silence (SPEC-09)

The Stage-4 demo moment, run end-to-end by the actual machinery. Against the R3m world (both approaches mined, the causeway dropped) no plan satisfies C2, C3 and C4 together — by construction (vignette §6) — and the dropped causeway forecloses C5 ("taken intact") for every plan, so C5 rides in every sacrifice set as a scenario-imposed, computed loss (SPEC-20). /relax does not pick one compromise and hide the trade: it returns every inclusion-minimal way forward, each naming what it gives up in command language. The sacrifices are computed — a commitment is sacrificed only when the SPEC-07 scorer returns violated for it — not authored. Ranked least-worst first by the commander's ordinal tiers (must / should / prefer, no numeric weight — DEC-19): the two should-sacrifices lead, the must-sacrifice (C2, "the strait opens two days late") is ranked last but present — never dropped, never silent (G4). Plans that give up more — {C2,C4,C5}, {C3,C4,C5} — are inclusion-dominated and never shown as separate options. relax stamp afffeecf288bac02… · 3 least-worst candidates over R3m, computed not authored · never empty, never a silent drop (G4) · tie-break stated (DEC-19) · each candidate cited_in the report back to named knowledge (G3)

  • least-worst #1 RX-fires C3 should C5 prefer
    Fires forward — suppress the FAC berths so the group crosses clean and on time, but the fires fall into the populated harbourfront where the craft berth among the fishing fleet. The Ledger causeway is already dropped in this branch; taking it intact is off the table.
    • gives up: No fires into the populated port district (C3)
    • gives up: The Ledger causeway is taken intact (C5)
  • least-worst #2 RX-parallel C4 should C5 prefer
    Parallel sweep — both approaches cleared at once, fastest to open the strait, but the amphibious group crosses the mined strait before the battery and FACs are suppressed. The Ledger causeway is already dropped in this branch; taking it intact is off the table.
    • gives up: Amphibious group not exposed to unsuppressed battery or FAC threat (C4)
    • gives up: The Ledger causeway is taken intact (C5)
  • least-worst #3 RX-sequential C2 must C5 prefer
    Sequential sweep — clear one approach then the other, keeping the group clear and the harbourfront quiet, but the strait opens at D+9, two days late. The Ledger causeway is already dropped in this branch; taking it intact is off the table.
    • gives up: Halcyon Strait swept and open to deep-draught traffic by D+7 (C2)
    • gives up: The Ledger causeway is taken intact (C5)

Tie-break — Same-tier sacrifices were ordered by commitment id (C3+C5 vs C4+C5) — a stated, commander-owned placeholder for within-tier priority, not a claim that one sacrifice outranks the other (ASSAY-DEC-19: no numeric weight; ties stated, never silent).

Scenario robustness — don't plan on most-likely (SPEC-10)

The Stage-5 demo moment (thesis C; JP 2-01.3: "don't plan on most-likely"). The generated handful scored across BASE, R1, R2, R3 — the adversary COA set from the vignette. Each cell is a four-stop verdict chip; a ▼ marks a verdict that dropped from its BASE value under that scenario ("collapse"). The worst column is the minimax: the plan's worst verdict for that commitment across all scenarios — a real verdict on a real scenario, not a weighted blend (DEC-15/19). Toggle R2 (Strait Denial) and watch the strait-early plan die on C1/C2 while robust alternatives hold. robustness stamp 0e216aabfed5f773… · 4 scenarios × 4 plans × 6 commitments · worst-case (minimax) verdict per plan×commitment · stamps_compatible=true

C1C2C3C4C5C6
plan \ scenario BASER1R2R3worstBASER1R2R3worstBASER1R2R3worstBASER1R2R3worstBASER1R2R3worstBASER1R2R3worst
P-SwSoCcXe · sweep-first · stand-off · contest · pull-early robust robust robust robust robust robust robust violated ▼ robust violated marginal marginal marginal marginal marginal tight tight robust tight tight marginal marginal marginal violated ▼ violated robust robust robust robust robust
P-SeFfCcXe · strait-early · fires-forward · contest · pull-early robust robust robust robust robust robust robust violated ▼ robust violated violated violated violated violated violated robust robust robust robust robust marginal marginal marginal violated ▼ violated robust robust robust robust robust
P-SeSoCcXe · strait-early · stand-off · contest · pull-early robust robust robust robust robust robust robust violated ▼ robust violated marginal marginal marginal marginal marginal tight tight robust tight tight marginal marginal marginal violated ▼ violated robust robust robust robust robust
P-SwFfCcXe · sweep-first · fires-forward · contest · pull-early robust robust robust robust robust robust robust violated ▼ robust violated violated violated violated violated violated robust robust robust robust robust marginal marginal marginal violated ▼ violated robust robust robust robust robust

Sensitivity — which beliefs bear load (SPEC-11)

The Stage-6 sensitivity demo moment (thesis E). Band-edge perturbation of each consumed knowledge object against the first handful plan: push each answer to its lo and hi edges, re-score, and count how many commitment verdicts change. K8 tops the ranking with single_source: true — its band-edge perturbation changes C4 verdicts via the battery fire-control threshold. The single_source flag is carried through from provenance, never used in the ranking arithmetic (DEC-19) — shown alongside so the commander sees the collection risk without it being laundered into a scalar. sensitivity stamp 55059bb7a57d38eb… · 9 knowledge objects perturbed · ranked by verdict-change count · single_source carried, not used in ranking (DEC-19)

Knowledge ID Changed Source Baseline Perturbed
K6 1 — robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust robustrobustmarginalmarginalmarginalrobust
K1 0 — robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust
K12a 0 — robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust
K2 0 — robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust
K3 0 — robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust
K4 0 — robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust
K7 0 — robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust
K8 0 single-source robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust
K9 0 — robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust robustrobustmarginaltightmarginalrobust

Discrimination — which questions separate COAs (SPEC-12)

The Stage-6 discrimination demo moment (thesis D). COA-pair band separation over open questions' expected-answer miniatures (DEC-18): for each open question with expected_answers, measure how well the answer bands separate each pair of adversary COAs. K11 ranks above K13 despite higher collection cost — the R1/R2 expected-answer bands are disjoint (mines loaded vs not), while K13's radio-traffic bands overlap across all COAs. Cost is shown alongside, never collapsed with value (DEC-19): the commander sees both and decides. No Shannon entropy, no VOI, no scenario weights (research note 08-analysis.md §3). discrimination stamp 9de62e0b97b4adce… · 2 open questions ranked · COA-pair separation over expected-answer bands (DEC-18) · cost alongside, never collapsed (DEC-19)

Question ID Best Separation Cost Per-pair Separation
K11 0.49999999999999994 fraction of stock staged 2–4 det-days R1 vs R20.49999999999999994 fraction of stock staged
R1 vs R30.3 fraction of stock staged
R2 vs R3-0.20000000000000007 fraction of stock staged
K13 -40 msgs/day 0.2–0.5 det-days R1 vs R2-40 msgs/day
R1 vs R3-50 msgs/day
R2 vs R3-50 msgs/day

Staleness — what goes stale when knowledge changes (SPEC-13)

The Stage-6 staleness demo moment (thesis F). Transitive forward trace walk from K9 (the storm-window knowledge that superseded K5): follow every downstream edge — consumed_by, scored_from, compiled_into — using the EDGE_ORIENTATION map from traceView.ts. The walk flags exactly the dependent verdicts and nothing else. It does not recompute — the constitution says flags, then humans decide. staleness stamp 90afb9dedfe9ea2f… · 98 verdicts + 1 worlds invalidated · transitive walk from K9 · flags only, no recompute (constitution)

444 trace chains walked, 444 complete
Worlds (1)
W-BASE
Verdicts (98)
